C1_LazyMotion_strict_motion_star.verification: Zero instances of `import { motion }` or `motion.*` elements remain. All 26 files migrated to `m.*` from `motion/react`. LazyMotion strict + domAnimation in providers.tsx confirmed.

H1_AnimatedStatCount_useReducedMotion.verification: AnimatedStatCount now imports useReducedMotion from motion/react, initializes useMotionValue to target when reduced, skips animate() call with early return, and includes prefersReduced in useEffect deps.

H2_DataTable_AnimatePresence_mode.verification: AnimatePresence changed from mode='popLayout' to mode='wait' with comment updated to match.

M1_Sidebar_spring_constants.verification: Sidebar.tsx imports EASING from animation-constants and uses EASING.spring for aside transition and EASING.snappy for layoutId active indicators.

M2_DataTable_animation_values.verification: DataTable rowVariants now use DURATION.normal and EASING.default. Skeleton/empty/data transitions use DURATION.fast. All imported from animation-constants.

M3_AnimatedTbody_ref_pattern.verification: AnimatedTbody now uses a callback ref pattern to merge inViewRef with external tbodyRef, replacing the fragile useEffect-based ref sync.

architecture_check.LazyMotion_strict: providers.tsx wraps app in <LazyMotion features={domAnimation} strict> — all components correctly use `m.*` not `motion.*`
architecture_check.MotionConfig_reducedMotion: providers.tsx includes <MotionConfig reducedMotion='user'> — global reduced motion respect
architecture_check.animation_constants_centralized: All animation values (DURATION, EASING, STAGGER, variants) centralized in lib/animation-constants.ts
architecture_check.no_migrate_calls: server.ts has no migrate() calls (comment only), values.yaml has migrate.enabled: false
architecture_check.e2e_parity: No E2E selectors reference motion.* elements (animations are internal implementation). Existing E2E tests (m-p3-features.spec.ts, optimistic-ui.spec.ts) remain valid.

summary: All 6 original findings (1 CRITICAL, 2 HIGH, 3 MEDIUM) are fully resolved. The fix commit is clean — purely mechanical migration from motion.* to m.*, plus targeted improvements for useReducedMotion, EASING constants, AnimatePresence mode, and callback ref. No new issues introduced. Phase 3 animation layer is production-ready.
